rules_evaluated.evidence: line:31 `Put the worktree at ../<repo-name>-m5 on a new branch named m5/ followed by the same task slug as the m4 branch.` and lines 34-35 `cp ../<repo-name>/CLAUDE.local.md ../<repo-name>-m5/` / `cp -r ../<repo-name>/.claude/memory/ ../<repo-name>-m5/.claude/memory/` — `<repo-name>` is a bracket-style placeholder inside a copyable fenced block
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Replace `<repo-name>` with an open-hook pattern: e.g., end the fenced block with `My repo name:` on its own line so the student fills it in before sending, or instruct Claude to derive the repo name from `basename $(pwd)` without a placeholder.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path, never by invented alias.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:68 `The reference, plan.md, and verifier are at the paths we wrote in this session.` — back-reference anchor present but no deterministic path; if paths were discussed mid-exercise without explicit file saves at named locations, the chain is not harness-testable and is vulnerable to path drift
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Name the deterministic paths explicitly in the re-send prompt (e.g., `reference.md`, `plan.md`, and the verifier at the path the build step wrote) rather than relying solely on scrollback recall.

rules_evaluated.evidence: line:10 `Laura Entis on Kieran Klaassen, You're the Bread in the AI Sandwich` — URL present but no `[practitioner analysis]` source-type label. line:10 `Kieran Klaassen, Compound Engineering: The Definitive Guide` — URL present but no `[practitioner direct]` label. line:90 `Kieran Klaassen, My AI Had Already Fixed the Code Before I Saw It` — URL present but no `[practitioner direct]` label.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Append the appropriate source-type label in square brackets after each URL or inline citation. Pre-reads section: [practitioner analysis] for the Entis article, [practitioner direct] for both Klaassen pieces.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Sessions are the unit of work.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:26 `open a fresh session in the original repo location` and line:40 `Open a new Claude Code session in the worktree` — correct session language used throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.
Minimal hand-off + Claude self-augments for send-off prompts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:72 `Before you start, ask for or write anything else you want for this run.` — canonical self-augment invitation present in re-send prompt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Smoke-test discipline belongs to the exercise file (diagnose-and-resend.md), not the module file. Module file correctly defers Phase 3 verifier-build to the exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts must be explicit at both ends.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Producing side: line:31 prompt names branch pattern `m4/` + `m5/<task-slug>` and worktree path `../<repo-name>-m5`. Consuming side (Module 6) is out of scope for this judge.

Entis article dated April 22, 2026 (within 6 months of 2026-05-03). Klaassen Compound Engineering dated February 9, 2026 (within 6 months). Klaassen My AI Had Already Fixed dated within 6 months (no explicit date in file but URL liveness confirmed).
